manage_design.when_to_use: Use for design-level lifecycle tasks, not geometry editing.

analyze_design.when_to_use: Use before editing when you need assembly structure, validation, or scene context.

create_sketch.when_to_use: Use to create a new sketch on a plane or on a body face before sketch editing or extrusion.
rules: Use body_name plus face_index when the sketch must be attached to an existing face.
The resolved target component is activated before sketch creation so the sketch lands in the intended assembly context.
If component_path is omitted for nested assemblies, Fusion may use the currently active component and place the sketch in the wrong location.

edit_sketch.when_to_use: Use to modify one existing sketch with multiple 2D operations in a single call.

apply_3d_features.when_to_use: Use for most solid modeling changes after a sketch exists.
rules: Prefer component_path on each operation when working in nested components.
Use target_body for Join/Cut when the target body is known.
Component-aware creation actions activate the resolved target component before creating bodies.
If component_path is omitted for nested assemblies, primitive creation may fall back to the wrong active component.

edit_assembly.when_to_use: Use for assembly structure and joints.
rules: Prefer component_path over component_name for nested assemblies.
For joints, prefer component1_path and component2_path.
Create components first, then use their component paths in later sketch or body creation so activation can target the correct assembly context.
After create_component, use the returned or expected component path in later calls instead of relying on the currently active component.

edit_surfaces.when_to_use: Use for grouped surface operations instead of direct solid modeling tools.
rules: Prefer component_path when the action references a sketch in a nested component.
Use body_names for stitch and body for offset or thicken.

edit_forms.when_to_use: Use for grouped T-Spline form operations.
rules: Use sketch plus dist for form extrusion.
Use body for display mode, symmetry, or conversion actions on existing T-Spline bodies.

import_mesh.when_to_use: Use to bring STL or OBJ geometry into the active Fusion design.

edit_mesh.when_to_use: Use for grouped mesh repair, smoothing, remesh, or conversion actions.
rules: Each action requires body and the target must already be a mesh body.
Use density for remesh, smoothing for smooth, repair_type for repair, and conv_type for convert.

list_parameters.when_to_use: Use before editing parameters when you need current parameter names or values.

edit_parameters.when_to_use: Use to create, update, or delete user parameters in one call.

export_model.when_to_use: Use only after the design is in the desired final state.
